message: Node started: Propose a geometric algorithm to partition a 10000x10000 square into N non-overlapping axis-aligned rectangles such that each rectangle i contains the point (x_i, y_i). Consider using a k-d tree approach or a recursive binary space partitioning (BSP) method where splits are chosen to keep the requested points within the resulting cells. Suggest how to adjust the final rectangle dimensions within each cell to approximate the target area r_i without overlapping or leaving the board.
